guest_info.name: Brian Keating
guest_info.title: Experimental Physicist at UCSD, Author, Podcaster

summary: Brian Keating discusses cosmology, astrophysics, and the search for extraterrestrial life. He delves into alternatives to the Big Bang theory, the significance of the cosmic microwave background, and the emotional journey of scientific discovery, including his own experiences with the BICEP2 experiment and the Nobel Prize.

main_entities.name: Telescope
main_entities.context: Discussed as a fundamental tool in astronomy and its historical impact.

main_entities.name: Big Bang Theory
main_entities.context: Discussed along with alternatives like cyclic cosmologies.

main_entities.name: Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB)
main_entities.context: Described as the oldest light in the universe and a key tool for cosmology.

main_entities.name: Inflation
main_entities.context: Discussed as a spark that ignited the Big Bang and its implications for the multiverse.

main_entities.name: BICEP2
main_entities.context: An experiment to detect primordial gravitational waves and the story of its announcement and subsequent retraction.

main_entities.name: Nobel Prize
main_entities.context: Discussed in the context of scientific ambition, recognition, and the human side of scientific discovery.

main_entities.name: Polarization
main_entities.context: The property of light used to detect gravitational waves in the CMB.

main_entities.name: Multiverse
main_entities.context: A consequence of inflationary cosmology and its philosophical implications.

main_entities.name: Gravitational Waves
main_entities.context: Used to confirm or deny inflation.

main_entities.name: Assayer Project
main_entities.context: Brian Keating's project to assess theories of everything with experiments.

main_entities.name: Space Dust
main_entities.context: The reason BICEP2 didn't win the Nobel Prize.

key_details.fact: The first telescope was invented by Hans Lippershey in the Netherlands, not Galileo.
The fusion and production of the lightest elements occurred in a time period shorter than the TV show, The Big Bang Theory.
Alternatives to the Big Bang theory include Aon theory and conformal cyclic cosmology.
The universe has approximately 44,000 square degrees.
The BICEP experiment is located at the Amundsen-Scott South Polar Station in Antarctica.
The CMB has 420 photons per cubic centimeter.
The signal from gravitational waves shaking up the CMB would be about one or two parts per billion of the CMB temperature itself.
The James Webb Space Telescope took 20 years to build and cost $9.7 billion.
